file.content:
# OWL Restrictions

ASG maps OWL class restrictions to OpenAPI schema constraints. This document covers all 17 restriction types recognized by ASG.

## Restriction Types Overview

| Category | OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
|----------|----------------|----------------|
| **Cardinality** | MinCardinality | `minItems`, required if ≥1 |
| | MaxCardinality | `maxItems` |
| | ExactCardinality | `minItems` + `maxItems` |
| **Value** | SomeValuesFrom | `minItems: 1`, required |
| | AllValuesFrom | Type constraint only |
| | HasValue | `default` value |
| | OneOf | `enum` (individuals) |
| **Data Cardinality** | DataMinCardinality | `minItems`, required if ≥1 |
| | DataMaxCardinality | `maxItems` |
| | DataExactCardinality | `minItems` + `maxItems` |
| **Data Value** | DataSomeValuesFrom | `minItems: 1`, required |
| | DataAllValuesFrom | Type constraint only |
| | DataHasValue | `default` value |
| | DataOneOf | `enum` (literals) |
| **Boolean** | UnionOf | `anyOf` |
| | IntersectionOf | `allOf` |
| | ComplementOf | `not` |

## Cardinality Restrictions

### MinCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets minimum number of values required.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasAddress"/>
    <owl:minCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:minCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasAddress:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
```

**Requiredness:** If `minCardinality ≥ 1` and `enforceRequiredness: true`, the property is added to `required`.

### MaxCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets maximum number of values allowed.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/primaryPhone"/>
    <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:maxCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
primaryPhone:
  type: array
  maxItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
```

**Note:** `maxCardinality: 1` still generates an array with `maxItems: 1`. For true singular properties, use `exactCardinality: 1` which ASG may optimize to a non-array.

### ExactCardinality (Object Properties)

Sets exact number of values required.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/supervisor"/>
    <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
    <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/Manager"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
supervisor:
  minItems: 1
  maxItems: 1
  # For cardinality 1, may be optimized to non-array
```

### DataMinCardinality / DataMaxCardinality / DataExactCardinality

Same as above but for data properties (literals).

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/phoneNumber"/>
    <owl:minCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:minCardinality>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
phoneNumber:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
```

## Value Restrictions

### SomeValuesFrom (Object Properties)

Requires at least one value from the specified class.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSkill"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Skill"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasSkill:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Skill'
```

**Requiredness:** Always implies required when `enforceRequiredness: true`.

### AllValuesFrom (Object Properties)

Constrains all values to be from the specified class (type constraint only).

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEmail"/>
    <owl:allValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmailAddress"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
hasEmail:
  type: array
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EmailAddress'
# No minItems - property is optional
```

**Note:** `allValuesFrom` does NOT imply cardinality. It only constrains the type when values exist.

### HasValue (Object Properties)

Requires a specific individual value.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
    <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/USA"/>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: "USA"
```

### OneOf (Object Properties - Enumeration)

Restricts to one of several named individuals.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
            </owl:oneOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
    - Pending
```

### DataSomeValuesFrom / DataAllValuesFrom / DataHasValue / DataOneOf

Same semantics as object property versions, but for literal values.

**DataOneOf Example (OWL):**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/priority"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <rdfs:Datatype>
            <owl:oneOf>
                <rdf:List>
                    <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">low</rdf:first>
                    <rdf:rest>
                        <rdf:List>
                            <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">medium</rdf:first>
                            <rdf:rest>
                                <rdf:List>
                                    <rdf:first rdf:datatype="xsd:string">high</rdf:first>
                                    <rdf:rest rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#nil"/>
                                </rdf:List>
                            </rdf:rest>
                        </rdf:List>
                    </rdf:rest>
                </rdf:List>
            </owl:oneOf>
        </rdfs:Datatype>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
priority:
  type: string
  enum:
    - low
    - medium
    - high
```

## Boolean Combinations

### UnionOf

Allows values from any of several classes.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
            </owl:unionOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
contact:
  anyOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
```

### IntersectionOf

Requires values satisfying all specified classes.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/seniorManager"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:intersectionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Manager"/>
                <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SeniorStaff"/>
            </owl:intersectionOf>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
seniorManager:
  allOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Manager'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/SeniorStaff'
```

### ComplementOf

Excludes values of a specified type.

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:Restriction>
    <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/nonManager"/>
    <owl:someValuesFrom>
        <owl:Class>
            <owl:complementOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/Manager"/>
        </owl:Class>
    </owl:someValuesFrom>
</owl:Restriction>
```

**OpenAPI Effect:**
```yaml
nonManager:
  not:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/Manager'
```

## Requiredness Summary

With `enforceRequiredness: true`, these restrictions make a property required:

| Restriction | Required? |
|-------------|-----------|
| MinCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| ExactCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| SomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| DataMinCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| DataExactCardinality(n) where n ≥ 1 | Yes |
| DataSomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| MaxCardinality | No |
| AllValuesFrom | No |
| HasValue | No |
| OneOf | No |

## Array Generation Summary

These restrictions cause array generation (rather than singular values):

| Restriction | Generates Array? |
|-------------|------------------|
| MinCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| MaxCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| ExactCardinality(n) where n > 1 | Yes |
| SomeValuesFrom | Yes |
| DataSomeValuesFrom | Yes |

1→# Property Mappings
     2→
     3→This document details how OWL data properties, object properties, and taxonomy properties are mapped to OpenAPI schemas.
     4→
     5→## Data Properties
     6→
     7→Data properties represent literal values (strings, numbers, dates, etc.).
     8→
     9→### Basic Mapping
    10→
    11→**OWL:**
    12→```xml
    13→<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/name">
    14→    <skos:prefLabel>Name</skos:prefLabel>
    15→    <skos:definition>The person's full name</skos:definition>
    16→</owl:DatatypeProperty>
    17→
    18→<!-- On the class -->
    19→<rdfs:subClassOf>
    20→    <owl:Restriction>
    21→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
    22→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    23→    </owl:Restriction>
    24→</rdfs:subClassOf>
    25→```
    26→
    27→**OpenAPI:**
    28→```yaml
    29→properties:
    30→  name:
    31→    type: string
    32→    title: Name
    33→    description: The person's full name
    34→    example: "name string value"
    35→required:
    36→  - name  # because of someValuesFrom
    37→```
    38→
    39→### Cardinality Effects
    40→
    41→| Restriction | Schema Effect |
    42→|-------------|---------------|
    43→| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
    44→| `allValuesFrom` | Type only, not required |
    45→| `exactCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
    46→| `minCardinality(1)` | `minItems: 1`, required |
    47→| `maxCardinality(1)` | `maxItems: 1` |
    48→| `minCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |
    49→
    50→### Enum from DataOneOf
    51→
    52→**OWL:**
    53→```xml
    54→<rdfs:subClassOf>
    55→    <owl:Restriction>
    56→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
    57→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
    58→            <rdfs:Datatype>
    59→                <owl:oneOf>
    60→                    <rdf:List>
    61→                        <rdf:first>active</rdf:first>
    62→                        <rdf:rest>
    63→                            <rdf:List>
    64→                                <rdf:first>inactive</rdf:first>
    65→                                <rdf:rest rdf:resource="rdf:nil"/>
    66→                            </rdf:List>
    67→                        </rdf:rest>
    68→                    </rdf:List>
    69→                </owl:oneOf>
    70→            </rdfs:Datatype>
    71→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    72→    </owl:Restriction>
    73→</rdfs:subClassOf>
    74→```
    75→
    76→**OpenAPI:**
    77→```yaml
    78→status:
    79→  type: string
    80→  enum:
    81→    - active
    82→    - inactive
    83→```
    84→
    85→### Default Values from DataHasValue
    86→
    87→**OWL:**
    88→```xml
    89→<rdfs:subClassOf>
    90→    <owl:Restriction>
    91→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
    92→        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    93→    </owl:Restriction>
    94→</rdfs:subClassOf>
    95→```
    96→
    97→**OpenAPI:**
    98→```yaml
    99→country:
   100→  type: string
   101→  default: USA
   102→```
   103→
   104→## Object Properties
   105→
   106→Object properties link to other classes (resources or non-resources).
   107→
   108→### Reference to Resource Class
   109→
   110→When the range is a class that is also a resource (has `apiID`), the property becomes a reference.
   111→
   112→**OWL:**
   113→```xml
   114→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   115→    <owl:Restriction>
   116→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employer"/>
   117→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Company"/>
   118→    </owl:Restriction>
   119→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   120→```
   121→
   122→**OpenAPI:**
   123→```yaml
   124→employer:
   125→  type: string
   126→  x-grl-model-link: Company
   127→  description: Unique identifier of Company
   128→  example: Company-12345
   129→```
   130→
   131→The `x-grl-model-link` extension indicates this is a reference to another API resource.
   132→
   133→### Reference to Non-Resource Class
   134→
   135→When the range is not a resource class, a `$ref` is used.
   136→
   137→**OWL:**
   138→```xml
   139→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   140→    <owl:Restriction>
   141→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
   142→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
   143→    </owl:Restriction>
   144→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   145→```
   146→
   147→**OpenAPI:**
   148→```yaml
   149→address:
   150→  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
   151→```
   152→
   153→### Array of References
   154→
   155→With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality > 1:
   156→
   157→**OWL:**
   158→```xml
   159→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   160→    <owl:Restriction>
   161→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employees"/>
   162→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
   163→    </owl:Restriction>
   164→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   165→```
   166→
   167→**OpenAPI:**
   168→```yaml
   169→employees:
   170→  type: array
   171→  minItems: 1
   172→  items:
   173→    type: string
   174→    x-grl-model-link: Employee
   175→    description: Unique identifier of Employee
   176→    example: Employee-12345
   177→```
   178→
   179→### Multiple Range Classes (Union)
   180→
   181→**OWL:**
   182→```xml
   183→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   184→    <owl:Restriction>
   185→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
   186→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   187→            <owl:Class>
   188→                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   189→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Person"/>
   190→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Organization"/>
   191→                </owl:unionOf>
   192→            </owl:Class>
   193→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   194→    </owl:Restriction>
   195→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   196→```
   197→
   198→**OpenAPI:**
   199→```yaml
   200→contact:
   201→  type: array
   202→  items:
   203→    type: object
   204→    additionalProperties: false
   205→    properties:
   206→      Person:
   207→        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Person'
   208→      Organization:
   209→        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Organization'
   210→```
   211→
   212→## Taxonomy Properties
   213→
   214→Taxonomy properties link to named individuals, creating enum-like constraints.
   215→
   216→### Individual-Based Enum
   217→
   218→**OWL:**
   219→```xml
   220→<!-- Named individuals -->
   221→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
   222→    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
   223→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   224→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
   225→    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
   226→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   227→
   228→<!-- Restriction referencing individuals -->
   229→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   230→    <owl:Restriction>
   231→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
   232→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   233→            <owl:Class>
   234→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   235→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
   236→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
   237→                </owl:oneOf>
   238→            </owl:Class>
   239→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   240→    </owl:Restriction>
   241→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   242→```
   243→
   244→**OpenAPI:**
   245→```yaml
   246→status:
   247→  type: string
   248→  enum:
   249→    - Active
   250→    - Inactive
   251→```
   252→
   253→### Class-Based Taxonomy
   254→
   255→When the restriction references a class, individuals of that class become enum values.
   256→
   257→**OWL:**
   258→```xml
   259→<!-- Taxonomy class -->
   260→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
   261→
   262→<!-- Individuals of the class -->
   263→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
   264→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
   265→    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
   266→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   267→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/PartTime">
   268→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
   269→    <skos:prefLabel>Part Time</skos:prefLabel>
   270→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   271→
   272→<!-- Restriction -->
   273→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   274→    <owl:Restriction>
   275→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
   276→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
   277→    </owl:Restriction>
   278→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   279→```
   280→
   281→**OpenAPI:**
   282→```yaml
   283→EmploymentStatus:
   284→  type: string
   285→  enum:
   286→    - Full Time
   287→    - Part Time
   288→```
   289→
   290→### Array of Taxonomy Values
   291→
   292→With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality indicating multiple values:
   293→
   294→**OpenAPI:**
   295→```yaml
   296→skills:
   297→  type: array
   298→  minItems: 1
   299→  items:
   300→    type: string
   301→    enum:
   302→      - Java
   303→      - Python
   304→      - JavaScript
   305→```
   306→
   307→## Property Name Resolution
   308→
   309→Property names are derived from the IRI:
   310→
   311→1. Use the fragment (after `#`) if present
   312→2. Otherwise use the last path segment
   313→3. CamelCase is preserved
   314→
   315→Examples:
   316→- `https://example.com/ontology#firstName` → `firstName`
   317→- `https://example.com/ontology/firstName` → `firstName`
   318→- `https://example.com/has_address` → `has_address`
   319→
   320→## Labels and Descriptions
   321→
   322→Properties get their metadata from:
   323→
   324→| Field | Source (priority order) |
   325→|-------|------------------------|
   326→| `title` | `skos:prefLabel`, `rdfs:label`, IRI short form |
   327→| `description` | `skos:definition`, `rdfs:comment` |
   328→| `example` | `skos:example` on axiom, `skos:example` on property, auto-generated |
   329→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

file.content:
# Property Mappings

This document details how OWL data properties, object properties, and taxonomy properties are mapped to OpenAPI schemas.

## Data Properties

Data properties represent literal values (strings, numbers, dates, etc.).

### Basic Mapping

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/name">
    <skos:prefLabel>Name</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>The person's full name</skos:definition>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<!-- On the class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
properties:
  name:
    type: string
    title: Name
    description: The person's full name
    example: "name string value"
required:
  - name  # because of someValuesFrom
```

### Cardinality Effects

| Restriction | Schema Effect |
|-------------|---------------|
| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
| `allValuesFrom` | Type only, not required |
| `exactCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
| `minCardinality(1)` | `minItems: 1`, required |
| `maxCardinality(1)` | `maxItems: 1` |
| `minCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |

### Enum from DataOneOf

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <rdfs:Datatype>
                <owl:oneOf>
                    <rdf:List>
                        <rdf:first>active</rdf:first>
                        <rdf:rest>
                            <rdf:List>
                                <rdf:first>inactive</rdf:first>
                                <rdf:rest rdf:resource="rdf:nil"/>
                            </rdf:List>
                        </rdf:rest>
                    </rdf:List>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </rdfs:Datatype>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - active
    - inactive
```

### Default Values from DataHasValue

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: USA
```

## Object Properties

Object properties link to other classes (resources or non-resources).

### Reference to Resource Class

When the range is a class that is also a resource (has `apiID`), the property becomes a reference.

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employer"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Company"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employer:
  type: string
  x-grl-model-link: Company
  description: Unique identifier of Company
  example: Company-12345
```

The `x-grl-model-link` extension indicates this is a reference to another API resource.

### Reference to Non-Resource Class

When the range is not a resource class, a `$ref` is used.

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
address:
  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
```

### Array of References

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality > 1:

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employees"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employees:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    x-grl-model-link: Employee
    description: Unique identifier of Employee
    example: Employee-12345
```

### Multiple Range Classes (Union)

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Person"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Organization"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
contact:
  type: array
  items:
    type: object
    additionalProperties: false
    properties:
      Person:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Person'
      Organization:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Organization'
```

## Taxonomy Properties

Taxonomy properties link to named individuals, creating enum-like constraints.

### Individual-Based Enum

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Named individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction referencing individuals -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
```

### Class-Based Taxonomy

When the restriction references a class, individuals of that class become enum values.

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>

<!-- Individuals of the class -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/PartTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Part Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
EmploymentStatus:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Full Time
    - Part Time
```

### Array of Taxonomy Values

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality indicating multiple values:

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
skills:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Java
      - Python
      - JavaScript
```

## Property Name Resolution

Property names are derived from the IRI:

1. Use the fragment (after `#`) if present
2. Otherwise use the last path segment
3. CamelCase is preserved

Examples:
- `https://example.com/ontology#firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/ontology/firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/has_address` → `has_address`

## Labels and Descriptions

Properties get their metadata from:

| Field | Source (priority order) |
|-------|------------------------|
| `title` | `skos:prefLabel`, `rdfs:label`, IRI short form |
| `description` | `skos:definition`, `rdfs:comment` |
| `example` | `skos:example` on axiom, `skos:example` on property, auto-generated |

   132→
   133→### String Enum Property
   134→
   135→```xml
   136→<!-- Named individuals as enum values -->
   137→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
   138→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
   139→    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
   140→</owl:NamedIndividual>
   141→<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
   142→    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>

   145→
   146→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   147→    <owl:Restriction>
   148→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
   149→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
   150→    </owl:Restriction>
   151→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   152→```
   153→
   154→**Result:** String property with enum: [Active, Inactive].
   155→
   156→### Reference to Another Resource
   157→
   158→```xml
   159→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   160→    <owl:Restriction>
   161→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdBy"/>
   162→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/User"/>
   163→    </owl:Restriction>

   167→**Result:** If `User` is a resource class:
   168→```yaml

   178→<!-- Address class (no apiID, not a resource) -->

   191→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   192→    <owl:Restriction>
   193→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
   194→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>

   227→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI"/>
   228→    </owl:Restriction>
   229→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   230→```
   231→
   232→**Result:**
   233→```yaml
   234→website:
   235→  type: string

   241→

   243→<rdfs:subClassOf>

   246→        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
   247→    </owl:Restriction>

   249→```
   250→
   251→**Result:**
   252→```yaml
   253→country:

   257→

   259→
   260→```xml
   261→<rdfs:subClassOf>
   262→    <owl:Restriction>
   263→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
   264→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   265→            <owl:Class>
   266→                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   267→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
   268→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
   269→                </owl:unionOf>
   270→            </owl:Class>
   271→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   272→    </owl:Restriction>
   273→</rdfs:subClassOf>
   274→```
   275→
   276→**Result:**
   277→```yaml
   278→contact:
   279→  anyOf:
   280→    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
   281→    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
   282→```
   283→
   284→## Security Patterns
   285→
   286→### OAuth2 with Per-Resource Scopes
   287→
   288→**config.yaml:**
   289→```yaml
   290→securitySchemeName: oauth2
   291→defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
   292→securityActionMappings:
   293→  GET: read
   294→  POST: create
   295→  PUT: update
   296→  PATCH: update
   297→  DELETE: delete
   298→
   299→templates:
   300→  openapi:
   301→    components:
   302→      securitySchemes:
   303→        oauth2:
   304→          type: oauth2
   305→          flows:
   306→            authorizationCode:
   307→              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
   308→              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
   309→```
   310→
   311→**Result:** Each endpoint gets security like `myapi.Employee.read`.
   312→
   313→### Resource-Level Security Override
   314→
   315→In ontology:
   316→```xml
   317→<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/AdminSettings">
   318→    <GRLAnnotations:security>admin</GRLAnnotations:security>
   319→</owl:Class>
   320→```
   321→
   322→**config.yaml:**
   323→```yaml
   324→securityTemplate: "{api}.{security}.{action}"
   325→```
   326→
   327→**Result:** AdminSettings endpoints get security like `myapi.admin.read`.
   328→
   329→### API Key Authentication
   330→
   331→```yaml
   332→templates:
   333→  openapi:
   334→    components:
   335→      securitySchemes:
   336→        api_key:
   337→          type: apiKey
   338→          in: header
   339→          name: X-API-Key
   340→    security:
   341→      - api_key: []
   342→
   343→# Disable scope-based security
   344→defaultSecurityTemplate: null
   345→```
   346→
   347→## Organization Patterns
   348→
   349→### Grouping Resources with Tags
   350→
   351→On `hasResourceSchema` axioms:
   352→```xml
   353→<owl:Axiom>
   354→    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
   355→    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
   356→    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Employee --></owl:annotatedTarget>
   357→    <api:apitag>Human Resources</api:apitag>
   358→</owl:Axiom>
   359→
   360→<owl:Axiom>
   361→    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
   362→    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
   363→    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Product --></owl:annotatedTarget>
   364→    <api:apitag>Inventory</api:apitag>
   365→</owl:Axiom>
   366→```
   367→
   368→**Result:** Resources grouped by tag in OpenAPI tags and operation tags.
   369→

file.content:
# Common Patterns

Recipe-style patterns for common ASG use cases.

## Configuration Patterns

### Multi-Environment Configuration

Use inheritance for environment-specific settings:

**base-config.yaml:**
```yaml
enforceRequiredness: true
namespaceMappings:
  - "!http://www.w3.org/.*"
  - ".*"
templates:
  openapi:
    openapi: 3.1.0
```

**dev-config.yaml:**
```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml
name: myapi_dev
outputDir: ./output/dev
templates:
  openapi:
    servers:
      - url: http://localhost:8080
        description: Development
```

**prod-config.yaml:**
```yaml
extends: base-config.yaml
name: myapi_prod
outputDir: ./output/prod
templates:
  openapi:
    servers:
      - url: https://api.example.com
        description: Production
```

### Multiple APIs from One Ontology

Define multiple API spec classes with different resources:

```yaml
# Public API
rootClass: https://example.com/PublicAPIV1.0
name: public_api
```

```yaml
# Admin API
rootClass: https://example.com/AdminAPIV1.0
name: admin_api
```

In the ontology, use different `apiLabel` values for each API.

## Ontology Patterns

### Required String Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required, non-array string property.

### Optional String Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/nickname"/>
        <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:maxCardinality>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Optional, non-array property (maxItems: 1).

### Required Array Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/tags"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required array with minItems: 1.

### Optional Array with Max Size

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/aliases"/>
        <owl:maxCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">5</owl:maxCardinality>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Optional array with maxItems: 5.

### Fixed-Size Array

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/coordinates"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">2</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#double"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Required array with minItems: 2, maxItems: 2.

### String Enum Property

```xml
<!-- Named individuals as enum values -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Status"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** String property with enum: [Active, Inactive].

### Reference to Another Resource

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdBy"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/User"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** If `User` is a resource class:
```yaml
createdBy:
  type: string
  x-grl-model-link: User
  description: Unique identifier of User
```

### Nested Object (Non-Resource)

```xml
<!-- Address class (no apiID, not a resource) -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Address">
    <skos:prefLabel>Address</skos:prefLabel>
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/street"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="xsd:string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
    <!-- more properties... -->
</owl:Class>

<!-- Reference from Person -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:** Nested object via $ref (not x-grl-model-link).

### DateTime Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/createdAt"/>
        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="xsd:nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
createdAt:
  type: string
  format: date-time
  example: "2025-01-15T14:55:58+00:00"
```

### URI Property

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/website"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
website:
  type: string
  format: uri
  example: "http://www.example.com"
```

### Property with Default Value

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: "USA"
```

### Union Type (anyOf)

```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Email"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Phone"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**Result:**
```yaml
contact:
  anyOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Email'
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/Phone'
```

## Security Patterns

### OAuth2 with Per-Resource Scopes

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
securitySchemeName: oauth2
defaultSecurityTemplate: "{api}.{model}.{action}"
securityActionMappings:
  GET: read
  POST: create
  PUT: update
  PATCH: update
  DELETE: delete

templates:
  openapi:
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        oauth2:
          type: oauth2
          flows:
            authorizationCode:
              authorizationUrl: https://auth.example.com/authorize
              tokenUrl: https://auth.example.com/token
```

**Result:** Each endpoint gets security like `myapi.Employee.read`.

### Resource-Level Security Override

In ontology:
```xml
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/AdminSettings">
    <GRLAnnotations:security>admin</GRLAnnotations:security>
</owl:Class>
```

**config.yaml:**
```yaml
securityTemplate: "{api}.{security}.{action}"
```

**Result:** AdminSettings endpoints get security like `myapi.admin.read`.

### API Key Authentication

```yaml
templates:
  openapi:
    components:
      securitySchemes:
        api_key:
          type: apiKey
          in: header
          name: X-API-Key
    security:
      - api_key: []

# Disable scope-based security
defaultSecurityTemplate: null
```

## Organization Patterns

### Grouping Resources with Tags

On `hasResourceSchema` axioms:
```xml
<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Employee --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <api:apitag>Human Resources</api:apitag>
</owl:Axiom>

<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="rdfs:subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- hasResourceSchema for Product --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <api:apitag>Inventory</api:apitag>
</owl:Axiom>
```

**Result:** Resources grouped by tag in OpenAPI tags and operation tags.

# Ontology Design Overview

To generate an OpenAPI specification, your ontology must follow specific structural patterns that ASG recognizes. This section covers all requirements for creating ASG-compatible ontologies.

## Documentation

- [Structure](structure.md) - Required structure (APIRoot, resources, properties)
- [Annotations](annotations.md) - All supported annotations
- [Restrictions](restrictions.md) - OWL restrictions and their OpenAPI mappings
- [Datatypes](datatypes.md) - XSD to OpenAPI type mapping

## Key Concepts

### API Identification

ASG identifies an API by finding a class that is a subclass of `APIRoot` with a `hasAPI` restriction. This class defines which resources are exposed in the API.

### Resource Classes

Resources are classes that become REST endpoints. Each resource class:
- Is linked via `hasResourceSchema` from the API class
- Has an `apiID` data property for unique identification
- Generates collection (`/Resource`) and single (`/Resource/{id}`) endpoints

### Property Mapping

Properties on resource classes become schema properties:

| OWL Property Type | OpenAPI Mapping |
|-------------------|-----------------|
| Data Property | Primitive type (string, number, etc.) |
| Object Property to Resource | `$ref` with `x-grl-model-link` |
| Object Property to Non-Resource | Inline object schema |
| Object Property with Individuals | Enum (taxonomy) |

### Annotation-Based Filtering

ASG uses the `apiLabel` annotation to filter which axioms are included in the generated API. Only restrictions annotated with the matching API label are processed.

## Required Imports

Your ontology should import the GRL base ontology which provides:

- `APIRoot` class
- `hasAPI` object property
- `hasResourceSchema` object property
- `apiID` data property
- Annotation properties

```xml
<owl:imports rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/V1.0.2"/>
```

## Minimal Example

```xml
<!-- API Root -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/GRLAPISpecifications"/>
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasResourceSchema"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Resource Class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Employee">
    <!-- Annotated restriction for apiID -->
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID"/>
            <owl:qualifiedCardinality>1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
            <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
    <!-- Annotated restriction for name property -->
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Annotation linking restriction to API -->
<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- blank node reference --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <www:apiLabel>MyAPIV1.0</www:apiLabel>
</owl:Axiom>
```

file.content:
# ASG Documentation

API Specification Generator (ASG) generates OpenAPI 3.1 specification files from OWL-RDF ontologies. It transforms semantic ontology structures into REST API specifications that can be used to generate API documentation, client and server code, and other tools.

## Quick Links

- [Getting Started](getting-started.md) - 5-minute setup guide
- [CLI Reference](cli-reference.md) - Command-line options
- [Configuration Reference](configuration/config-reference.md) - All YAML configuration options

## Documentation Sections

### Configuration

- [Configuration Overview](configuration/index.md) - How configuration works
- [Configuration Reference](configuration/config-reference.md) - Complete YAML option reference
- [Templates](configuration/templates.md) - OpenAPI template customization
- [Security](configuration/security.md) - Security scheme configuration

### Ontology Design

- [Ontology Requirements](ontology-design/index.md) - What your ontology needs
- [Structure](ontology-design/structure.md) - Required structure (APIRoot, resources)
- [Annotations](ontology-design/annotations.md) - All supported annotations
- [Restrictions](ontology-design/restrictions.md) - OWL restrictions to OpenAPI mapping
- [Datatypes](ontology-design/datatypes.md) - XSD to OpenAPI type mapping

### Mapping Reference

- [OWL to OpenAPI Matrix](mapping-reference/owl-to-openapi-matrix.md) - Quick reference
- [Property Mappings](mapping-reference/property-mappings.md) - Data/Object/Taxonomy properties

### Examples

- [Minimal Example](examples/minimal-example.md) - Simplest working configuration
- [Common Patterns](examples/common-patterns.md) - Recipe-style patterns

### Troubleshooting

- [Troubleshooting Guide](troubleshooting.md) - Common issues and solutions

## Prerequisites

- Java 21 or later
- Maven 3.6.3 or later
- An ontology in OWL RDF format
- A configuration file in YAML format

## Output

ASG generates the following files in the output directory:

```
output/<api-name>/
├── effective-config.yaml    # Merged configuration
├── ontology0.owl           # Original ontology copy
├── ontology-flat.owl       # Namespace-flattened
├── ontology-merged.owl     # All imports merged
├── openapi.json            # Generated spec (JSON)
└── openapi.yaml            # Generated spec (YAML)
```

input.content:
# Taxonomy Patterns

ASG detects and maps four distinct taxonomy patterns from OWL ontologies to OpenAPI schemas. Each pattern represents a different way to model enumerated or categorized values.

## Pattern Overview

| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output | Use Case |
|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|
| **A** | `owl:oneOf` with untyped individuals | Inline string enum | Simple, closed enumeration |
| **B** | `owl:oneOf` with typed individuals | Inline string enum | Closed enumeration with metadata |
| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema | Extensible, hierarchical taxonomy |
| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum | Standards-compliant vocabulary |

## Pattern Detection

ASG automatically detects the appropriate pattern based on the OWL structure:

```
OWL Restriction
├── owl:oneOf (blank node)?
│   ├── Individuals have domain class? → Pattern B
│   └── Untyped individuals only → Pattern A
└── Named class IRI?
    ├── Subclass of skos:Concept or has skos:inScheme? → Pattern D
    └── No object property restrictions? → Pattern C
```

---

## Pattern A: Inline Enumeration (Untyped Individuals)

Use Pattern A for simple, closed enumerations where you just need a list of valid values.

### When to Use

- Fixed set of values that rarely changes
- No metadata needed per value
- Values are simple labels

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Untyped individuals (only owl:NamedIndividual) -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Active">
    <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive">
    <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending">
    <rdfs:label>Pending</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasStatus"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
hasStatus:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Active
      - Inactive
      - Pending
```

### Cardinality Variations

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
|----------------|----------------|
| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
| `allValuesFrom` | `type: array` (optional) |
| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array (optional) |
| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |

---

## Pattern B: Typed Individuals with Metadata

Use Pattern B when you need individuals to have a domain class and potentially carry metadata like code values, definitions, or display names.

### When to Use

- Values need to be typed (instances of a specific class)
- Values may have metadata (codeValue, displayName, definition)
- You want to query individuals via SPARQL using their type

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Domain class for the enum values -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode">
    <skos:prefLabel>Sensor Type Code</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Typed individuals with metadata -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Analog</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Analog sensor type.</skos:definition>
    <example:codeValue>analog</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Digital</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Digital sensor type.</skos:definition>
    <example:codeValue>digital</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSensorType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
hasSensorType:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Analog
      - Digital
```

### Pattern A vs B Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern A | Pattern B |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Individual typing | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Domain class + metadata |
| SPARQL query | Parse `owl:oneOf` | `?x a SensorTypeCode` |
| Metadata support | `rdfs:label` only | Full annotation support |
| API filtering | Not supported | Via `apiLabel` on individuals |

---

## Pattern C: Class-Based Taxonomy (Extensible)

Use Pattern C for extensible taxonomies where values can be added without modifying the restriction, and optionally support hierarchical structure.

### When to Use

- Values should be discoverable via class membership
- New values can be added without changing the schema
- Hierarchical grouping is needed (via subclasses)
- You want a reusable schema in components/schemas

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Parent taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode">
    <skos:prefLabel>Environment Code</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Operating environment classification.</skos:definition>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Subclasses for hierarchy (optional) -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Indoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Individuals (instances of subclasses) -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOffice">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Office</skos:prefLabel>
    <example:codeValue>office</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOutdoor">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor</skos:prefLabel>
    <example:codeValue>outdoor</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to named class (NOT owl:oneOf) -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEnvironment"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
# On the resource
hasEnvironment:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EnvironmentCode'

# In components/schemas
EnvironmentCode:
  type: object
  properties:
    code:
      type: string
      enum:
        - office
        - outdoor
    displayName:
      type: string
```

### Flat vs Hierarchical

Pattern C supports both flat taxonomies (direct instances of the class) and hierarchical taxonomies (instances of subclasses).

**Flat Taxonomy:**
```
EnvironmentCode
├── EnvOffice (instance)
├── EnvWarehouse (instance)
└── EnvOutdoor (instance)
```

**Hierarchical Taxonomy:**
```
EnvironmentCode
├── IndoorEnvironment (subclass)
│   ├── EnvOffice (instance)
│   └── EnvWarehouse (instance)
└── OutdoorEnvironment (subclass)
    └── EnvOutdoor (instance)
```

### Pattern B vs C Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern B | Pattern C |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Restriction target | Blank node with `owl:oneOf` | Named class IRI |
| Set closure | Closed (explicit list) | Open (query instances) |
| Adding values | Requires changing `owl:oneOf` | Just add instance |
| Hierarchy | Flat only | Supports subclass hierarchy |
| OpenAPI output | Inline enum | `$ref` to schema |

---

## Pattern D: SKOS Taxonomy

Use Pattern D for standards-compliant vocabularies using SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System), which enables interoperability with terminology servers and FHIR-compatible systems.

### When to Use

- Standards compliance required (SKOS, FHIR)
- Multi-language support needed (`skos:prefLabel` with `@lang`)
- Hierarchical relationships (`skos:broader`, `skos:narrower`)
- External code mapping (`skos:exactMatch`)
- Terminology server integration

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- SKOS ConceptScheme -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code System</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Bridge class linking to SKOS scheme -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <owl:equivalentClass>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme"/>
            <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </owl:equivalentClass>
    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- SKOS Concepts -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextContinuous">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
    <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
    <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Continuous measurement context.</skos:definition>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextDiscrete">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
    <skos:notation>discrete</skos:notation>
    <skos:prefLabel>Discrete</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Discrete measurement context.</skos:definition>
    <skos:narrower rdf:resource="https://example.com/ContextTriggered"/>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to SKOS-based class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasMeasurementContext"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
MeasurementContextCode:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Continuous
      - Discrete
      - Triggered
```

### SKOS Detection Criteria

ASG detects Pattern D when the target class:
- Is `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`, OR
- Has an `owl:equivalentClass` with a `skos:inScheme` restriction

### Pattern C vs D Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern C (Class-based) | Pattern D (SKOS) |
|--------|-------------------------|------------------|
| Standard | OWL only | OWL + SKOS |
| Hierarchy | `rdfs:subClassOf` | `skos:broader/narrower` |
| Membership | `rdf:type` | `skos:inScheme` |
| Multi-language | Not native | `skos:prefLabel@en` |
| External mapping | Not native | `skos:exactMatch` |
| Discovery | `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* Class` | `?x skos:inScheme Scheme` |

---

## Cardinality Summary

All patterns support the same cardinality restrictions:

| OWL Restriction | Required? | Array? | minItems | maxItems |
|-----------------|-----------|--------|----------|----------|
| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Yes | No | - | - |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | No | No | - | - |
| `someValuesFrom` | Yes | Yes | 1 | - |
| `allValuesFrom` | No | Yes | - | - |
| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | Yes (if n≥1) | Yes | n | - |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(n)` | No | Yes | - | n |

---

## Choosing the Right Pattern

| Requirement | Recommended Pattern |
|-------------|---------------------|
| Simple closed list, rarely changes | Pattern A |
| Typed values with metadata | Pattern B |
| Extensible, add values without schema change | Pattern C |
| Hierarchical grouping needed | Pattern C or D |
| Standards compliance (SKOS, FHIR) | Pattern D |
| Multi-language support | Pattern D |
| External terminology mapping | Pattern D |

toolUseResult.content:
# Taxonomy Patterns

ASG detects and maps four distinct taxonomy patterns from OWL ontologies to OpenAPI schemas. Each pattern represents a different way to model enumerated or categorized values.

## Pattern Overview

| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output | Use Case |
|---------|---------------|----------------|----------|
| **A** | `owl:oneOf` with untyped individuals | Inline string enum | Simple, closed enumeration |
| **B** | `owl:oneOf` with typed individuals | Inline string enum | Closed enumeration with metadata |
| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema | Extensible, hierarchical taxonomy |
| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum | Standards-compliant vocabulary |

## Pattern Detection

ASG automatically detects the appropriate pattern based on the OWL structure:

```
OWL Restriction
├── owl:oneOf (blank node)?
│   ├── Individuals have domain class? → Pattern B
│   └── Untyped individuals only → Pattern A
└── Named class IRI?
    ├── Subclass of skos:Concept or has skos:inScheme? → Pattern D
    └── No object property restrictions? → Pattern C
```

---

## Pattern A: Inline Enumeration (Untyped Individuals)

Use Pattern A for simple, closed enumerations where you just need a list of valid values.

### When to Use

- Fixed set of values that rarely changes
- No metadata needed per value
- Values are simple labels

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Untyped individuals (only owl:NamedIndividual) -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Active">
    <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive">
    <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending">
    <rdfs:label>Pending</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasStatus"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Active"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Inactive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Pending"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
hasStatus:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Active
      - Inactive
      - Pending
```

### Cardinality Variations

| OWL Restriction | OpenAPI Effect |
|----------------|----------------|
| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
| `allValuesFrom` | `type: array` (optional) |
| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | Non-array (optional) |
| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |

---

## Pattern B: Typed Individuals with Metadata

Use Pattern B when you need individuals to have a domain class and potentially carry metadata like code values, definitions, or display names.

### When to Use

- Values need to be typed (instances of a specific class)
- Values may have metadata (codeValue, displayName, definition)
- You want to query individuals via SPARQL using their type

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Domain class for the enum values -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode">
    <skos:prefLabel>Sensor Type Code</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Typed individuals with metadata -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Analog</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Analog sensor type.</skos:definition>
    <example:codeValue>analog</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/SensorTypeCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Digital</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Digital sensor type.</skos:definition>
    <example:codeValue>digital</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasSensorType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/SensorTypeDigital"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
hasSensorType:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Analog
      - Digital
```

### Pattern A vs B Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern A | Pattern B |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Individual typing | `owl:NamedIndividual` only | Domain class + metadata |
| SPARQL query | Parse `owl:oneOf` | `?x a SensorTypeCode` |
| Metadata support | `rdfs:label` only | Full annotation support |
| API filtering | Not supported | Via `apiLabel` on individuals |

---

## Pattern C: Class-Based Taxonomy (Extensible)

Use Pattern C for extensible taxonomies where values can be added without modifying the restriction, and optionally support hierarchical structure.

### When to Use

- Values should be discoverable via class membership
- New values can be added without changing the schema
- Hierarchical grouping is needed (via subclasses)
- You want a reusable schema in components/schemas

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- Parent taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode">
    <skos:prefLabel>Environment Code</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Operating environment classification.</skos:definition>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Subclasses for hierarchy (optional) -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Indoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Individuals (instances of subclasses) -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOffice">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/IndoorEnvironment"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Office</skos:prefLabel>
    <example:codeValue>office</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/EnvOutdoor">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/OutdoorEnvironment"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor</skos:prefLabel>
    <example:codeValue>outdoor</example:codeValue>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to named class (NOT owl:oneOf) -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasEnvironment"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EnvironmentCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
# On the resource
hasEnvironment:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EnvironmentCode'

# In components/schemas
EnvironmentCode:
  type: object
  properties:
    code:
      type: string
      enum:
        - office
        - outdoor
    displayName:
      type: string
```

### Flat vs Hierarchical

Pattern C supports both flat taxonomies (direct instances of the class) and hierarchical taxonomies (instances of subclasses).

**Flat Taxonomy:**
```
EnvironmentCode
├── EnvOffice (instance)
├── EnvWarehouse (instance)
└── EnvOutdoor (instance)
```

**Hierarchical Taxonomy:**
```
EnvironmentCode
├── IndoorEnvironment (subclass)
│   ├── EnvOffice (instance)
│   └── EnvWarehouse (instance)
└── OutdoorEnvironment (subclass)
    └── EnvOutdoor (instance)
```

### Pattern B vs C Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern B | Pattern C |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| Restriction target | Blank node with `owl:oneOf` | Named class IRI |
| Set closure | Closed (explicit list) | Open (query instances) |
| Adding values | Requires changing `owl:oneOf` | Just add instance |
| Hierarchy | Flat only | Supports subclass hierarchy |
| OpenAPI output | Inline enum | `$ref` to schema |

---

## Pattern D: SKOS Taxonomy

Use Pattern D for standards-compliant vocabularies using SKOS (Simple Knowledge Organization System), which enables interoperability with terminology servers and FHIR-compatible systems.

### When to Use

- Standards compliance required (SKOS, FHIR)
- Multi-language support needed (`skos:prefLabel` with `@lang`)
- Hierarchical relationships (`skos:broader`, `skos:narrower`)
- External code mapping (`skos:exactMatch`)
- Terminology server integration

### OWL Structure

```xml
<!-- SKOS ConceptScheme -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code System</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Bridge class linking to SKOS scheme -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <owl:equivalentClass>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme"/>
            <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </owl:equivalentClass>
    <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:Class>

<!-- SKOS Concepts -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextContinuous">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
    <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
    <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Continuous measurement context.</skos:definition>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/ContextDiscrete">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
    <skos:notation>discrete</skos:notation>
    <skos:prefLabel>Discrete</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>Discrete measurement context.</skos:definition>
    <skos:narrower rdf:resource="https://example.com/ContextTriggered"/>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to SKOS-based class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/hasMeasurementContext"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/MeasurementContextCode"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

### OpenAPI Output

```yaml
MeasurementContextCode:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Continuous
      - Discrete
      - Triggered
```

### SKOS Detection Criteria

ASG detects Pattern D when the target class:
- Is `rdfs:subClassOf skos:Concept`, OR
- Has an `owl:equivalentClass` with a `skos:inScheme` restriction

### Pattern C vs D Comparison

| Aspect | Pattern C (Class-based) | Pattern D (SKOS) |
|--------|-------------------------|------------------|
| Standard | OWL only | OWL + SKOS |
| Hierarchy | `rdfs:subClassOf` | `skos:broader/narrower` |
| Membership | `rdf:type` | `skos:inScheme` |
| Multi-language | Not native | `skos:prefLabel@en` |
| External mapping | Not native | `skos:exactMatch` |
| Discovery | `?x a/rdfs:subClassOf* Class` | `?x skos:inScheme Scheme` |

---

## Cardinality Summary

All patterns support the same cardinality restrictions:

| OWL Restriction | Required? | Array? | minItems | maxItems |
|-----------------|-----------|--------|----------|----------|
| `qualifiedCardinality(1)` | Yes | No | - | - |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(1)` | No | No | - | - |
| `someValuesFrom` | Yes | Yes | 1 | - |
| `allValuesFrom` | No | Yes | - | - |
| `minQualifiedCardinality(n)` | Yes (if n≥1) | Yes | n | - |
| `maxQualifiedCardinality(n)` | No | Yes | - | n |

---

## Choosing the Right Pattern

| Requirement | Recommended Pattern |
|-------------|---------------------|
| Simple closed list, rarely changes | Pattern A |
| Typed values with metadata | Pattern B |
| Extensible, add values without schema change | Pattern C |
| Hierarchical grouping needed | Pattern C or D |
| Standards compliance (SKOS, FHIR) | Pattern D |
| Multi-language support | Pattern D |
| External terminology mapping | Pattern D |

# Ontology Design Overview

To generate an OpenAPI specification, your ontology must follow specific structural patterns that ASG recognizes. This section covers all requirements for creating ASG-compatible ontologies.

## Documentation

- [Structure](structure.md) - Required structure (APIRoot, resources, properties)
- [Annotations](annotations.md) - All supported annotations
- [Restrictions](restrictions.md) - OWL restrictions and their OpenAPI mappings
- [Datatypes](datatypes.md) - XSD to OpenAPI type mapping

## Key Concepts

### API Identification

ASG identifies an API by finding a class that is a subclass of `APIRoot` with a `hasAPI` restriction. This class defines which resources are exposed in the API.

### Resource Classes

Resources are classes that become REST endpoints. Each resource class:
- Is linked via `hasResourceSchema` from the API class
- Has an `apiID` data property for unique identification
- Generates collection (`/Resource`) and single (`/Resource/{id}`) endpoints

### Property Mapping

Properties on resource classes become schema properties:

| OWL Property Type | OpenAPI Mapping |
|-------------------|-----------------|
| Data Property | Primitive type (string, number, etc.) |
| Object Property to Resource | `$ref` with `x-grl-model-link` |
| Object Property to Non-Resource | Inline object schema |
| Object Property with Individuals | Enum (taxonomy) |

### Annotation-Based Filtering

ASG uses the `apiLabel` annotation to filter which axioms are included in the generated API. Only restrictions annotated with the matching API label are processed.

## Required Imports

Your ontology should import the GRL base ontology which provides:

- `APIRoot` class
- `hasAPI` object property
- `hasResourceSchema` object property
- `apiID` data property
- Annotation properties

```xml
<owl:imports rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/V1.0.2"/>
```

## Minimal Example

```xml
<!-- API Root -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/MyAPIV1.0">
    <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/GRLAPISpecifications"/>
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasResourceSchema"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Resource Class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/Employee">
    <!-- Annotated restriction for apiID -->
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID"/>
            <owl:qualifiedCardinality>1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
            <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
    <!-- Annotated restriction for name property -->
    <rdfs:subClassOf>
        <owl:Restriction>
            <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
            <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
        </owl:Restriction>
    </rdfs:subClassOf>
</owl:Class>

<!-- Annotation linking restriction to API -->
<owl:Axiom>
    <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
    <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
    <owl:annotatedTarget><!-- blank node reference --></owl:annotatedTarget>
    <www:apiLabel>MyAPIV1.0</www:apiLabel>
</owl:Axiom>
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ASG Documentation

API Specification Generator (ASG) generates OpenAPI 3.1 specification files from OWL-RDF ontologies. It transforms semantic ontology structures into REST API specifications that can be used to generate API documentation, client and server code, and other tools.

## Quick Links

- [Getting Started](getting-started.md) - 5-minute setup guide
- [CLI Reference](cli-reference.md) - Command-line options
- [Configuration Reference](configuration/config-reference.md) - All YAML configuration options

## Documentation Sections

### Configuration

- [Configuration Overview](configuration/index.md) - How configuration works
- [Configuration Reference](configuration/config-reference.md) - Complete YAML option reference
- [Templates](configuration/templates.md) - OpenAPI template customization
- [Security](configuration/security.md) - Security scheme configuration

### Ontology Design

- [Ontology Requirements](ontology-design/index.md) - What your ontology needs
- [Structure](ontology-design/structure.md) - Required structure (APIRoot, resources)
- [Annotations](ontology-design/annotations.md) - All supported annotations
- [Restrictions](ontology-design/restrictions.md) - OWL restrictions to OpenAPI mapping
- [Datatypes](ontology-design/datatypes.md) - XSD to OpenAPI type mapping

### Mapping Reference

- [OWL to OpenAPI Matrix](mapping-reference/owl-to-openapi-matrix.md) - Quick reference
- [Property Mappings](mapping-reference/property-mappings.md) - Data/Object/Taxonomy properties

### Examples

- [Minimal Example](examples/minimal-example.md) - Simplest working configuration
- [Common Patterns](examples/common-patterns.md) - Recipe-style patterns

### Troubleshooting

- [Troubleshooting Guide](troubleshooting.md) - Common issues and solutions

## Prerequisites

- Java 21 or later
- Maven 3.6.3 or later
- An ontology in OWL RDF format
- A configuration file in YAML format

## Output

ASG generates the following files in the output directory:

```
output/<api-name>/
├── effective-config.yaml    # Merged configuration
