input.old_string:
## Taxonomy Properties

Taxonomy properties link to named individuals, creating enum-like constraints.

### Individual-Based Enum

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Named individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction referencing individuals -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
```

### Class-Based Taxonomy

When the restriction references a class, individuals of that class become enum values.

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>

<!-- Individuals of the class -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/PartTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Part Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
EmploymentStatus:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Full Time
    - Part Time
```

### Array of Taxonomy Values

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality indicating multiple values:

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
skills:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Java
      - Python
      - JavaScript
```
input.new_string:
## Taxonomy Properties

Taxonomy properties link to named individuals or classes, creating enum-like constraints. ASG supports four taxonomy patterns with different characteristics.

For comprehensive documentation on all taxonomy patterns, see [Taxonomy Patterns](../ontology-design/taxonomy-patterns.md).

### Quick Reference

| Pattern | OWL Structure | OpenAPI Output |
|---------|---------------|----------------|
| **A** | `owl:oneOf` + untyped individuals | Inline string enum |
| **B** | `owl:oneOf` + typed individuals | Inline string enum |
| **C** | Named class (no SKOS) | `$ref` to schema |
| **D** | SKOS-based class | Inline string enum |

### Pattern A: Inline Enumeration

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Untyped individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction with owl:oneOf -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Active
      - Inactive
```

### Pattern C: Class-Based Taxonomy

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>

<!-- Typed individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction to named class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employmentType:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    $ref: '#/components/schemas/EmploymentStatus'

# In components/schemas
EmploymentStatus:
  type: object
  properties:
    code:
      type: string
      enum:
        - Full Time
    displayName:
      type: string
```

### Choosing a Pattern

- **Simple closed list**: Pattern A
- **Typed values with metadata**: Pattern B
- **Extensible taxonomy**: Pattern C
- **SKOS/FHIR compliance**: Pattern D

See [Taxonomy Patterns](../ontology-design/taxonomy-patterns.md) for detailed examples of all patterns.

<!-- Untyped individuals -->

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Property Mappings

This document details how OWL data properties, object properties, and taxonomy properties are mapped to OpenAPI schemas.

## Data Properties

Data properties represent literal values (strings, numbers, dates, etc.).

### Basic Mapping

**OWL:**
```xml
<owl:DatatypeProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/name">
    <skos:prefLabel>Name</skos:prefLabel>
    <skos:definition>The person's full name</skos:definition>
</owl:DatatypeProperty>

<!-- On the class -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/name"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
properties:
  name:
    type: string
    title: Name
    description: The person's full name
    example: "name string value"
required:
  - name  # because of someValuesFrom
```

### Cardinality Effects

| Restriction | Schema Effect |
|-------------|---------------|
| `someValuesFrom` | `type: array`, `minItems: 1`, required |
| `allValuesFrom` | Type only, not required |
| `exactCardinality(1)` | Non-array, required |
| `minCardinality(1)` | `minItems: 1`, required |
| `maxCardinality(1)` | `maxItems: 1` |
| `minCardinality(n)` where n>1 | `type: array`, `minItems: n`, required |

### Enum from DataOneOf

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <rdfs:Datatype>
                <owl:oneOf>
                    <rdf:List>
                        <rdf:first>active</rdf:first>
                        <rdf:rest>
                            <rdf:List>
                                <rdf:first>inactive</rdf:first>
                                <rdf:rest rdf:resource="rdf:nil"/>
                            </rdf:List>
                        </rdf:rest>
                    </rdf:List>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </rdfs:Datatype>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - active
    - inactive
```

### Default Values from DataHasValue

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/country"/>
        <owl:hasValue rdf:datatype="xsd:string">USA</owl:hasValue>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
country:
  type: string
  default: USA
```

## Object Properties

Object properties link to other classes (resources or non-resources).

### Reference to Resource Class

When the range is a class that is also a resource (has `apiID`), the property becomes a reference.

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employer"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Company"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employer:
  type: string
  x-grl-model-link: Company
  description: Unique identifier of Company
  example: Company-12345
```

The `x-grl-model-link` extension indicates this is a reference to another API resource.

### Reference to Non-Resource Class

When the range is not a resource class, a `$ref` is used.

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/address"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Address"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
address:
  $ref: '#/components/schemas/Address'
```

### Array of References

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality > 1:

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employees"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/Employee"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
employees:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    x-grl-model-link: Employee
    description: Unique identifier of Employee
    example: Employee-12345
```

### Multiple Range Classes (Union)

**OWL:**
```xml
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/contact"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:unionOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Person"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/Organization"/>
                </owl:unionOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
contact:
  type: array
  items:
    type: object
    additionalProperties: false
    properties:
      Person:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Person'
      Organization:
        $ref: '#/components/schemas/Organization'
```

## Taxonomy Properties

Taxonomy properties link to named individuals, creating enum-like constraints.

### Individual-Based Enum

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Named individuals -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Active</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive">
    <skos:prefLabel>Inactive</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction referencing individuals -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/status"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom>
            <owl:Class>
                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusActive"/>
                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/StatusInactive"/>
                </owl:oneOf>
            </owl:Class>
        </owl:someValuesFrom>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
status:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Active
    - Inactive
```

### Class-Based Taxonomy

When the restriction references a class, individuals of that class become enum values.

**OWL:**
```xml
<!-- Taxonomy class -->
<owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>

<!-- Individuals of the class -->
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/FullTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Full Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>
<owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/PartTime">
    <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    <skos:prefLabel>Part Time</skos:prefLabel>
</owl:NamedIndividual>

<!-- Restriction -->
<rdfs:subClassOf>
    <owl:Restriction>
        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/employmentType"/>
        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/EmploymentStatus"/>
    </owl:Restriction>
</rdfs:subClassOf>
```

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
EmploymentStatus:
  type: string
  enum:
    - Full Time
    - Part Time
```

### Array of Taxonomy Values

With `someValuesFrom` or cardinality indicating multiple values:

**OpenAPI:**
```yaml
skills:
  type: array
  minItems: 1
  items:
    type: string
    enum:
      - Java
      - Python
      - JavaScript
```

## Property Name Resolution

Property names are derived from the IRI:

1. Use the fragment (after `#`) if present
2. Otherwise use the last path segment
3. CamelCase is preserved

Examples:
- `https://example.com/ontology#firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/ontology/firstName` → `firstName`
- `https://example.com/has_address` → `has_address`

## Labels and Descriptions

Properties get their metadata from:

| Field | Source (priority order) |
|-------|------------------------|
| `title` | `skos:prefLabel`, `rdfs:label`, IRI short form |
| `description` | `skos:definition`, `rdfs:comment` |
| `example` | `skos:example` on axiom, `skos:example` on property, auto-generated |
